sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.71 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: For a ring$R$, we say that a group$G$is of type$(R)$if the trivial$RG$-module$R$admits a finite resolution by finitely generated free modules. If$R$is a field, we define the Euler characteristic of$G$over$R$to be the alternating sum of$R$-ranks of homology groups$_i(G;R)$.
